understanding.goal_understanding: Complete each level by getting every goal tile marked with a '+' to be occupied by a selectable piece at the same time (if there are multiple goals, all must be covered simultaneously) before the step budget runs out.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: There is an active/selected piece. MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN attempts to move the selected piece through the grid but movement can be blocked by walls/obstacles, and it appears to slide until it hits a blocker (e.g., MOVE_UP moved from [30,21] to [30,18] in one action). CLICK can reposition the selected piece to the clicked coordinate only when the move is permitted by the level geometry/rules (e.g., clicking [12,21] successfully moved from [30,21] to [12,21], but many other clicks failed due to blocking/invalid destinations). When an action is blocked, the piece stays in place but still consumes a step.

orient_history: Movement appears grid/step-based with collisions; since all moves are blocked, progress likely requires using CLICK to reposition/select or interact (possibly teleport/place a selectable onto the goal by clicking it).

CLICK likely changes selection when clicking another selectable, and may attempt to move/teleport the currently selected piece to the clicked location (but can fail if blocked/invalid). Since movement is blocked, switching selection is the next observable test.

CLICK likely performs a direct placement/teleport (or other special move) independent of the arrow-move collision checks; earlier CLICK on the goal may have failed due to clicking the goal’s edge/incorrect point. If CLICK can place onto goal, clicking near the goal center should move the selected piece onto it and satisfy the win condition.

Clicks can select/move a selectable to the clicked location if the destination is reachable/valid; movement actions shift the selected piece by its size (3 px / 1 cell) but are blocked by walls. Since the currently selected piece is fully surrounded, progress likely requires selecting the other selectable (at [9,21]) to maneuver it onto the goal region.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to place/move the currently selected 3x3 piece to the clicked pixel position, but it only succeeds when the target aligns to the 3-pixel grid (multiples of 3) and doesn’t collide with walls. The earlier click on [35,17] failed because it is not grid-aligned.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): CLICK does not directly move onto the goal reliably; instead, standard MOVE_* commands are the primary way to slide the currently selected piece by 1 cell (3 pixels) within free space, and CLICK is mainly for selecting/teleporting between predefined selectable anchor positions. Since I must switch action type now, I will test MOVE to see whether it changes position, selection, or reveals a different movement rule despite the 'blocked' flags.

CLICK likely switches control/teleports selection to the clicked selectable (as previously observed when clicking [18,21] and [9,21] moved the 'selected piece' position), while MOVE_* attempts to step 3 pixels (1 cell) but is currently blocked for this piece.

CLICK appears to change selection when clicking on a selectable; MOVE_* moves the currently selected piece by one cell (3 px) if not blocked by walls. To make progress, switch to the other selectable and see if it has an open move path.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a reachable/occupied cell (especially another selectable) can either (a) move the active piece along the same row/column until the target, and/or (b) switch which selectable is controlled. Since the current piece is boxed in for MOVE, I should try controlling the other selectable to see if it has a path toward the goal pocket.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches which piece is selected (shown as the 'player' position changing). Only the currently selected piece responds to MOVE_*; different pieces may have different mobility depending on their pocket. Need to select the movable piece and navigate it into the goal region.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is active (the log reports this as the 'selected piece moved' because the selected entity changes), while MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) if not blocked by walls.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): CLICK moves the currently-selected piece to a clicked reachable/valid cell (possibly within a confined pocket), but this selected piece at [18,21] is fully boxed-in so it cannot traverse toward the goal via normal movement from here. Normal MOVE actions step by 1 cell (3 px) when not blocked; if blocked, nothing happens. To make progress we likely need to get back to a position where MOVE is possible or later switch to the other selectable and navigate it.

CLICK appears to attempt a straight-line (same x or same y) reposition to the clicked coordinate; diagonal clicks have no effect. It’s unclear whether CLICK can jump over intervening walls, but it may if alignment is correct.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects the piece at the clicked position (changing which piece is controlled), while MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) but can be blocked by walls/other selectables. To make progress we should select the other piece and test its available moves.

CLICK likely either selects a piece at the clicked position or attempts to move the currently-selected piece to that position; to make progress we need to move/select the other selectable at [15,21] to clear the right side so [12,21] can advance toward the goal.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is controlled when clicking on another selectable (and may also attempt to move if clicking empty reachable space). Since the currently selected piece at [15,21] cannot move, the only progress is to switch control to the other selectable at [12,21] and probe its possible moves/lines.

CLICK appears to move the selected piece to the clicked pixel location when that destination is valid; if clicking a cell occupied by the other selectable it likely swaps their positions. MOVE_* attempts shift by one cell (3 px) and is blocked by walls/other selectables. To reach the goal area, we likely need to use CLICK to relocate the selected piece out of the boxed-in pocket toward the right.

Discarding prior click-teleport hypothesis. New hypothesis: MOVE actions against an adjacent selectable may trigger an interaction (e.g., swap selection, swap positions, or attempt a push-chain), even when normal movement is reported as blocked.

CLICK likely either (a) attempts to move the currently-selected piece directly to the clicked position if a clear path/placement is allowed, or (b) selects a different selectable when clicking on it. Since P is walled-in, we may need to switch selection to S to reach the goal.

CLICK seems to attempt relocating the selected piece to the clicked pixel position, but the move only succeeds if the destination is a valid non-wall landing spot (possibly the exact goal object footprint). Since direct movement into the adjacent goal cell is blocked, the most promising next step is clicking directly on the goal object's coordinates.

CLICK likely either (a) moves the selected piece to the clicked grid-aligned cell if empty, or (b) switches selection if clicking on another selectable. Previous CLICKs that were grid-aligned and on empty space moved; non-grid-aligned (e.g., [35,17]) seemed to do nothing. So I should CLICK a grid-aligned goal cell.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is active (and may also attempt a move if clicking a reachable empty tile). Since the currently-selected piece is immobile, switching control to the other selectable is the best way to make progress toward occupying the goal area.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is active (and may be logged as a 'move'); arrow-key MOVE actions then attempt to move the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) unless blocked by walls.

I discard the idea that repeated CLICK positioning alone will solve this from the current spot. Based on observations, the piece can sometimes relocate via CLICK, but collision is strict and the current placement is hard-locked by surrounding walls/geometry; I need to probe movement rules with a non-CLICK action to find any allowed slide/push behavior.

CLICK appears to attempt to relocate the selected piece directly to the clicked pixel position if that footprint is not blocked by collidable walls (it fails when the destination is blocked). It’s unclear whether clicking directly on a goal tile is allowed; we need to test placing onto the goal-adjacent cell.

CLICK likely interacts based on the exact pixel point clicked (e.g., selecting/activating within an object's bounds). Prior CLICKs at exact object corners (e.g., [35,17]) may have missed the goal's active area; trying the goal's interior (near its center) may allow snapping/placement onto the goal despite MOVE collisions.

CLICK may allow direct placement/teleport to a clicked cell (not only switching between selectables), but it seems sensitive to exact coordinates; prior clicks inside/near the goal ([35,17]) may have missed the actionable anchor (likely the top-left of a goal cell). If I can CLICK the top-left of a goal cell, the selected piece might move onto the goal despite MOVE being blocked by corner collision.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): Movement is grid-stepped by 3 pixels (piece size 3x3). The goal area is adjacent but the selected piece is misaligned relative to a wall boundary, so rightward entry is blocked unless we change vertical alignment first. Even if the immediate up move is blocked, testing a different movement axis can reveal if there is any vertical gap/slide available with this piece size/collision.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* cannot enter goal tiles (treated as non-walkable for movement), but CLICK can reposition the selected 3x3 piece onto a valid 3x3-aligned top-left coordinate; winning requires the 3x3 piece to exactly cover the 3x3 goal region, which corresponds to pixel [33,15].

CLICK teleports the selected selectable to the clicked pixel position if that destination area is valid (not colliding with walls/blocks). Goals are non-collidable, so clicking inside the goal region should place the piece onto it to satisfy the win condition for this single goal.

CLICK sometimes relocates the selected piece to the clicked tile/region if it is a valid destination (e.g., empty/goal), otherwise nothing happens. Since movement is fully blocked, the only remaining chance is to CLICK directly on the goal object's position to attempt placement onto the goal.

Actions (including CLICK) have been consuming steps, and with steps_remaining=0 we likely cannot move/teleport pieces anymore. If any action is still accepted at 0 steps, the only possible way to win would be to trigger a placement/win check by interacting with the goal.

history.summary: Level 1 step budget exhausted.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: Complete each level by getting every goal tile marked with a '+' to be occupied by a selectable piece at the same time (if there are multiple goals, all must be covered simultaneously) before the step budget runs out.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: There is an active/selected piece. MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN attempts to move the selected piece through the grid but movement can be blocked by walls/obstacles, and it appears to slide until it hits a blocker (e.g., MOVE_UP moved from [30,21] to [30,18] in one action). CLICK can reposition the selected piece to the clicked coordinate only when the move is permitted by the level geometry/rules (e.g., clicking [12,21] successfully moved from [30,21] to [12,21], but many other clicks failed due to blocking/invalid destinations). When an action is blocked, the piece stays in place but still consumes a step.
